sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 1.90 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.1)

statement.latex: A subgroup$H$of a group$G$is called$2$-infi\-ni\-te\-ly isolated\/ in$G$if, whenever the centralizer$C_G(h)$in$G$of some element$h 1$of$H$contains at least one involution and intersects$H$in an infinite subgroup, it follows that$C_G(h) H$.
